title: Parameters, rest and return contracts

publication.changeSummary: Technology-specific JavaScript: Language and Browser Applications draft for Parameters, rest and return contracts; independent standards, browser, pedagogy, and accessibility review required. Beginner-quality contract 1.0.0 applied deterministically from existing source-linked draft facts; publication stage and reviewer state are unchanged.

sources.locator: ECMAScript Language Specification; living specification accessed 2026-07; provisions applicable to Parameters, rest and return contracts
sources.claim: Parameters receive argument values and can use defaults, rest collection, and destructuring with explicit validation.

sources.locator: MDN JavaScript Guide; accessed 2026-07; provisions applicable to Parameters, rest and return contracts
sources.claim: Parameters receive argument values and can use defaults, rest collection, and destructuring with explicit validation.

canonicalExplanation.summary: Parameters receive argument values and can use defaults, rest collection, and destructuring with explicit validation.

sections.heading: Start here
sections.body: Start with this case: A function uses options = {} before destructuring optional properties. Your goal is to design and test a function with a clear parameter and failure contract. Work through the example one step at a time, then use the boundary to check your answer.

sections.heading: Before you begin
sections.body: Review these declared prerequisites first: Values, types and expressions; pf abstraction calls; Function declarations, expressions and arrows; let, const and binding. If one is unfamiliar, revisit it before continuing.

sections.heading: Technology rule
sections.body: Parameters receive argument values and can use defaults, rest collection, and destructuring with explicit validation. Apply this rule to the actual markup, stylesheet, JavaScript, or component state rather than memorizing its name.

sections.heading: Boundary and browser reality
sections.body: Defaults apply only to undefined, objects remain shared references, and destructuring can throw on nullish input. This draft therefore rejects: A default parameter runs for null and every other falsy argument.

includes: Use defaults, destructuring, rest parameters and explicit return behavior without shared mutable defaults.
Design and test a function with a clear parameter and failure contract.
excludes: Defaults apply only to undefined, objects remain shared references, and destructuring can throw on nullish input.

outcomes: Design and test a function with a clear parameter and failure contract.

examples.title: Parameters, rest and return contracts: concrete case
examples.situation: A function uses options = {} before destructuring optional properties.
walkthrough: Step 1 — Inspect the exact artifact and starting state: A function uses options = {} before destructuring optional properties.
Step 2 — Use the core rule above to predict one observable result.
Step 3 — Verify the correction: Parameter defaults apply when the argument is undefined, not for null or all falsy values.
examples.result: The result follows because Parameter defaults apply when the argument is undefined, not for null or all falsy values.
